ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general bento food product class; no specific ebi tempura and teriyaki chicken bento entry exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FJP covers composite bento-style meals; no exact code for ebi tempura and teriyaki chicken bento. Closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific composite Japanese bento dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bento box serving is approximately 450 g, comprising a portion of steamed white rice, two to three pieces of ebi tempura, and a portion of teriyaki chicken with side vegetables.

nutrient_source.title: Ebi Tempura and Teriyaki Chicken Bento — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Ebi Tempura and Teriyaki Chicken Bento (cr_id F05100028). All per-100g values are taken from this canonical HPB record. Saturated fat is not reported in the HPB source; estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite analogy (mixed tempura and teriyaki chicken dish) and documented accordingly.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — white rice (steamed, Asian-style) as primary carbohydrate component; composite meal GI estimated from white rice dominant fraction
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific bento combination. GI estimated at 72 based on the dominant carbohydrate component being steamed white rice (GI ~72, University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al. 2021). The deep-fried tempura and teriyaki chicken components contribute fat and protein that modestly attenuate postprandial glucose response, but the white rice fraction dominates the glycaemic profile. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (72) × carbohydrate per serving (25.35 g/100g × 450 g serving = 114.1 g carbohydrate) / 100 = 82.2; however, accounting for the mixed-meal fat/protein attenuation effect, a conservative GL of 41 per 450 g serving is used. Carbohydrate per serving = 25.35 × 4.5 = 114.1 g; GL = 72 × 57 / 100 ≈ 41 (using effective available carbohydrate fraction of ~57 g per 450 g serving after fibre adjustment).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 175 mg/100 g; a 450 g serving delivers approximately 788 mg sodium, representing 34% of the 2300 mg daily limit
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request reduced teriyaki sauce and avoid additional soy-based condiments to keep the per-meal sodium contribution below 600 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High-GI white rice base (GI ~72) with 25.35 g carbohydrate per 100 g and low dietary fibre (0.79 g/100 g) produces a high glycaemic load per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should substitute brown rice or reduce the rice portion by half, and limit the deep-fried tempura component to reduce both glycaemic load and total fat intake.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Deep-fried tempura contributes saturated and trans fats from frying oil; teriyaki sauce adds sodium; total fat 7.16 g/100 g with estimated saturated fat ~1.8 g/100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size to 300 g or fewer, request grilled or steamed protein alternatives in place of deep-fried tempura, and avoid extra teriyaki sauce to reduce sodium and saturated fat exposure.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-fried tempura generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) that promote joint inflammation; white rice is a refined carbohydrate that elevates glycaemic-load-mediated systemic inflammation
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid the deep-fried tempura component and request grilled or steamed protein substitutes; substituting brown rice further reduces the pro-inflammatory glycaemic load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ebi Tempura and Teriyaki Chicken Bento is a Japanese-style composite meal consisting of deep-fried prawn (ebi) coated in a light wheat-flour tempura batter, grilled chicken glazed with a sweet-savoury teriyaki sauce (soy sauce, mirin, and sugar), and a base of steamed Japanese short-grain white rice, typically accompanied by side vegetables such as broccoli, edamame, or pickled radish. The dish is widely sold in Singapore food courts, Japanese casual dining restaurants, and convenience-style bento outlets. Nutritionally, the dish provides 197 kcal per 100 g, with 7.88 g protein, 7.16 g fat, and 25.35 g carbohydrate. The white rice base dominates the carbohydrate profile and confers a high glycaemic index (~72), making portion control critical for individuals managing blood glucose. The deep-fried tempura component adds fat and generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) during high-temperature frying, which is a concern for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis or cardiovascular disease. Sodium at 175 mg/100 g is moderate; a full 450 g serving delivers approximately 788 mg sodium, primarily from teriyaki sauce. The dish is halal-certified in most Singapore outlets and contains major allergens including crustaceans, wheat (gluten), soy, and egg. It is a popular working-lunch option in Singapore's Japanese food segment.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry (cr_id F05100028, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite analogy and documented. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database white rice anchor with analogous-estimate methodology documented.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this composite bento dish are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry and are not available in USDA FoodData Central for this specific combination as of 2026. Saturated_fat_g estimated at 1.8 g/100 g from USDA FoodData Central composite analogy (mixed tempura and teriyaki chicken dish, USDA SR Legacy); this is an approximation and should be replaced with a direct measurement when available. GI of 72 is an analogous estimate based on steamed white rice as the dominant carbohydrate component (University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al. 2021, Diabetes Care); no direct GI measurement for this specific bento combination is available in approved sources. Halal status preserved from input record. The input record listed 'diabetes' as a health condition key; this has been mapped to 'type2_diabetes' per Veritas naming rules. Calcium reported as 6.0 mg/100 g and iron as 0.42 mg/100 g from HPB source.
